record.description: Term names the act of domineering over others as an exercise of power — the inner being is engaged through the disposition of the one who dominates (absence of the fear of God) and the moral contrast with restrained, God-fearing authority

record.description: Term names the multiplication of sin, transgression, iniquity, evil, and abomination — the inner being morally corrupted to an escalating degree
record.context_description: Term names the multiplication of sin, transgression, iniquity, evil, and abomination — the inner being morally corrupted to an escalating degree

record.description: Term names the multiplication of spiritual unfaithfulness — whoring after other gods, multiplying idolatrous altars, and escalating apostasy that provokes divine anger
record.context_description: Term names the multiplication of spiritual unfaithfulness — whoring after other gods, multiplying idolatrous altars, and escalating apostasy that provokes divine anger

record.description: Term names the multiplication of inner-being suffering — pain, sorrow, mourning, vexation, distress, fear, and grief intensified to an overwhelming degree
record.context_description: Term names the multiplication of inner-being suffering — pain, sorrow, mourning, vexation, distress, fear, and grief intensified to an overwhelming degree

record.description: Term names the multiplication of wisdom, knowledge, understanding, and their counterparts — foolish excess of words, vanity of multiplied study, the breadth of mind God grants, and the limits of multiplied inquiry

record.description: Term names the multiplication of divine grace — abundant pardon, plentiful redemption, enlargement of the inner being through divine gentleness, and divinely multiplied strength for the weary

record.description: Term names multiplication as moral and covenantal consequence — increase predicated on obedience, prayer, inner posture, or wisdom reception; also divine multiplication of visions, signs, and words addressed to the inner being
record.context_description: Term names multiplication as moral and covenantal consequence — increase predicated on obedience, prayer, inner posture, or wisdom reception; also divine multiplication of visions, signs, and words addressed to the inner being

record.description: Term names the growth of power and pride — addressed through prophetic vision as a warning to the inner being of the sovereign; the growing tree is the parabolic image of self-magnifying pride confronted by God

record.description: Term names greatness as divinely-granted sovereign power — the inner being engaged through the confrontation of pride, the experience of humiliation and restoration, the fear it produces in others, and the eschatological hope of its transfer to God's people

record.description: Term names the exercise of dominion — the inner being is engaged through the creational mandate to rule with responsibility, the character-revealing contrast between ruthless and righteous dominion, the experience of subjugation under oppressive rule, the messianic hope for just dominion, and the penetrating descent of divine judgment into the bones

record.description: Term names the imperious, shameless inner disposition — a heart so sick with spiritual adultery that it acts with brazen boldness in its unfaithfulness
record.context_description: Term names the imperious, shameless inner disposition — a heart so sick with spiritual adultery that it acts with brazen boldness in its unfaithfulness

record.description: Term applied to God's sovereign rule over kingdoms — the inner being engaged through the repeated, humbling recognition that all human authority is derivative and that God gives power to whom he will; the great king's journey from pride to acknowledgement

record.description: Term names divine might as the limit of human contention — the inner being confronted with the wisdom of submission before one stronger
record.context_description: Term names divine might as the limit of human contention — the inner being confronted with the wisdom of submission before one stronger

record.description: Term names the prevailing of a superior force over a person — the inner being engaged through the wisdom of companionship against adversity, the recognition of divine prevailing power over mortal life, and the overwhelming of the soul by distress and anguish

record.description: Term names royal might as the object of pride — the inner being's self-glorification in its own power, at the very moment of divine judgment

record.description: Term names might as divinely bestowed — all royal power has its source in the God of heaven who gives and withholds; the inner being receives this as theological orientation
record.context_description: Term names might as divinely bestowed — all royal power has its source in the God of heaven who gives and withholds; the inner being receives this as theological orientation

record.description: Term names the governor's exercise of authority at the boundary of the sacred — the inner being engaged through rulings on holy access, the conscience about sacred participation, and the pastoral-authoritative word that redirects communal grief toward holy joy
record.context_description: Term names the governor's exercise of authority at the boundary of the sacred — the inner being engaged through rulings on holy access, the conscience about sacred participation, and the pastoral-authoritative word that redirects communal grief toward holy joy

record.description: Term names the receiving of what is due — whether divine inheritance, adopted sonship, or inward penalty; the inner being engaged as recipient of consequence or promise
record.context_description: Term names the receiving of what is due — whether divine inheritance, adopted sonship, or inward penalty; the inner being engaged as recipient of consequence or promise

record.description: Term names the becoming of the inner being — transformation, formation, identity-shift, disclosure, and accountability; including becoming like Christ, becoming mature or childish, becoming a participant in good or evil, and the becoming-disclosed of the heart secrets
record.context_description: Term names the becoming of the inner being — transformation, formation, identity-shift, disclosure, and accountability; including becoming like Christ, becoming mature or childish, becoming a participant in good or evil, and the becoming-disclosed of the heart secrets

record.description: Term names the act of demonstrating — the inner being engaged as source or recipient of what is shown: love, earnestness, hope, conscience, divine power and wrath made visible through demonstration
record.context_description: Term names the act of demonstrating — the inner being engaged as source or recipient of what is shown: love, earnestness, hope, conscience, divine power and wrath made visible through demonstration

record.description: Term names the inner-being goal of winning others — the motivating purpose behind Paul's self-subordination; love for others expressed as the drive to gain them for salvation
record.context_description: Term names the inner-being goal of winning others — the motivating purpose behind Paul's self-subordination; love for others expressed as the drive to gain them for salvation

record.description: Term as the particle of inner-being warning — it marks the feared spiritual failure that vigilance must prevent: drifting, an unbelieving heart, falling short of rest
record.context_description: Term as the particle of inner-being warning — it marks the feared spiritual failure that vigilance must prevent: drifting, an unbelieving heart, falling short of rest

record.description: Term qualifies, grounds, and intensifies inner-being orientations — the particle marks how inner states are to be reckoned, how they arise from theological realities, and how they shape conduct; including self-examination, faith, conscience, apostasy, love, forgiveness, submission, ambition, and the reckoning of death to sin and life to God

record.description: Term names the filling or completing of the inner being — whether filled with joy, comfort, wisdom, knowledge, the Spirit, divine fullness, or moral corruption; and the fulfilment of calling, law, and obedience through love and Spirit-empowered character
record.context_description: Term names the filling or completing of the inner being — whether filled with joy, comfort, wisdom, knowledge, the Spirit, divine fullness, or moral corruption; and the fulfilment of calling, law, and obedience through love and Spirit-empowered character

record.description: Term names the sifting of the inner being by a hostile spiritual agent — the adversarial trial to which faith is subjected

record.description: Term names the coming or approach that engages the inner being through encounter with God — God coming to humans in theophany, warning, judgment and salvation; humans coming before God in prayer, worship, confession, and sacred entry; the day of the Lord approaching with dread or hope

record.description: Term names the arrival of calamity, distress, dread, and mortality upon the inner being — the moment when feared things come, sighing replaces bread, evil comes instead of hoped-for good, and trouble arrives without rest

record.description: Term in relational and covenantal entry with inner-being moral dimension — entering another person with longing, deception, desperation, or transgression; entering covenant commitment; and the purity implications of entering sacred or defiled space

record.description: Term names divine might as the object of praise and the source of human capacity — the inner being engaged through doxological recognition and grateful reception of divinely-given strength
